channel.title: Parla Releases
channel.description: Sparkle update feed for Parla — local-first macOS dictation + meeting recorder.

item.title: Parla 2.24
item.description: v2.24 — Phase 2: browser-hosted meeting detection. The existing native meeting-app detector (Zoom.app, Teams.app, Webex.app, Discord, Slack) now has a companion that polls running browsers' active tab URLs every 3 seconds and fires the SAME 'Record this meeting?' prompt when it finds a meeting URL. Detected URL patterns: meet.google.com/<3-4-3 letter code>, zoom.us/(j|wc|w)/, teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join, teams.live.com/meet, *.webex.com/(meet|join), whereby.com/, around.co/r/, discord.com/channels/.... Browsers covered: Chrome (+canary +beta), Safari (+TP), Arc, Brave, Edge, Vivaldi, Opera. Firefox excluded — doesn't expose URL via AppleScript. Each meeting URL only fires once; repeated polls don't re-prompt. Gated on the same calendarIntegrationEnabled flag as the native detector.

item.title: Parla 2.23
item.description: v2.23 — privacy + bug fixes. (1) Public DMG no longer leaks the developer's home-folder path via MLX C++ debug strings. The 25 `/Users//...` strings that were visible to anyone running `strings Parla` are now scrubbed via a post-build pass (build.sh → scripts/scrub-binary.py). The cert-embedded developer name will be cleaned in a later opt-in release (regenerating the signing cert requires re-granting macOS TCC permissions, so it's a deliberate user choice). (2) Settings → Help → 'Open Meeting Browser' button now actually opens the browser. Same root cause as the Sparkle bug we fixed in v2.20: `NSApp.delegate as? AppDelegate` returns nil under SwiftUI's @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor in Settings-only scenes. Routed through a new StatusBarController.shared singleton (matching the SparkleUpdater.shared pattern). The 'Open Welcome' button in the same panel got the same fix.
